rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Of Petitioner Donald Trammell By And Through His Gal Clinton Killians Memorandum In Support Of Order Directing Compliance With Subpoena Duces Tecum
rulings.Rulings: This motion will be continued to September 30, 2020, so as to be heard after the motions currently set for hearing on September 24, 2020 in this case.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PETER B. BLASKO TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Sanctions; Memorandum In Support
rulings.Rulings: Respondents' Motion to Compel Further Responses and for Sanctions is granted. Petitioner must produce non-privileged documents, and a privilege log if applicable responsive, to Special Interrogatories Number One and Two, within fourteen days of entry of this order. Sanctions are awarded to the Respondents and against Petitioner under CCP § 2023.030, in the amount of $2,460. Sanctions to be paid within fourteen days of entry of this order. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3772) no later than 4:00 p.m. on August 5, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PETER B. BLASKO TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondents Briana Anderson Blaskos, John Pickerings And Christine Heyl Wagners Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Notice To Briana Anderson Blasko, For Stay Of Deposition And For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Respondents' Motion to Quash Deposition Notice to Briana Anderson Blasko, for Stay of Deposition and for Protective Order is denied. Respondents have made an inadequate showing on which to find that the notice of deposition should be quashed. The Court declines to quash the notice of deposition. Respondents have not satisfied the burden established in CCP sec. symbol 2025.420 for issuance of a protective order regarding the production of non-privileged documents responsive to Petitioner's Amended Request for Production of Documents Set One. The Court declines to issue a protective order. It appears to the Court that the parties have spent an insufficient effort meeting and conferring regarding the discovery dispute prior to bringing the matter before the Court. The parties are encouraged to engage in a robust meet and confer process in light of this tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3772) no later than 4:00 p.m. on August 5, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PETER B. BLASKO TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner's Motion to Compel production of documents in response to Amended Request for Production of Documents Set One is granted, pursuant to CCP § 2031.320. The production must comply with CCP § 2031.280. Respondents are directed to produce responsive, non-duplicate, non-privileged documents within fourteen days of entry of this order. Absent agreement between the parties otherwise, a privilege log must be produced for any document withheld on the basis of privilege, within fourteen days of entry of this order. Respondents do not dispute that they are in possession of responsive non-privileged documents. There is insufficient showing that production of the relevant non-privileged electronically stored information would be from a source that is not reasonably accessible due to undue burden or expense. There is an inadequate showing that the projected potential cost of production is undue in consideration of the needs of the case, the amount in controversy, and the importance of the issues at stake in the litigation. Accordingly, the Court does not find good cause to allocate the expense of discovery under CCP §§ 2025.420(e). Sanctions are awarded to Petitioner and against Respondents pursuant to CCP § 2031.320 in the amount of $2,460, as the Court does not find that Respondents acted with substantial justification in withholding the responsive non-privileged documents or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. Sanctions to be paid within fourteen days of entry of this order. The parties are encouraged to further meet and confer in light of this tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3772) no later than 4:00 p.m. on August 5, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.
